title: Pack Your Bag for the Trail

setup.situation: packing a daypack for a family hike

root.narration: Your family is going on a three-hour hike in the mountains tomorrow. Your parent says you can pack your own small backpack. You have a water bottle, a granola bar, a rain jacket, sunscreen, a whistle, and your tablet. Your backpack can only fit five things. What do you pack?

choices.text: Water bottle, granola bar, rain jacket, sunscreen, and whistle.
Water bottle, granola bar, rain jacket, tablet, and sunscreen.
Tablet, granola bar, water bottle, and two extra snacks from the kitchen.

outcome.headline: Trail-Ready Pack!
outcome.explanation: You picked all the right things. Water keeps you from getting dehydrated. A snack gives you energy. The rain jacket protects you if the weather changes -- mountain weather can surprise you. Sunscreen protects your skin. And the whistle is a safety tool: if you ever get separated from your group, three short blasts on a whistle is the signal for HELP and carries much farther than your voice.
outcome.whatToRemember: The five hiking essentials for kids: WATER, FOOD, RAIN LAYER, SUN PROTECTION, and a WHISTLE. A whistle is louder than yelling and can help rescuers find you.

keyTerms.definition: The universal signal for help. If you are lost, blow your whistle three times, wait, and repeat.
A small backpack with the supplies you need for a day hike. Always pack the essentials, even for short hikes.

outcome.headline: Almost There!
outcome.explanation: You got most of the essentials right -- water, food, rain jacket, and sunscreen are all great choices. But a tablet is not useful on a trail. It can break, get wet, and it does not help in an emergency. A whistle would have been a better pick because it can save your life if you get separated from your group.
outcome.whatToRemember: Leave electronics at home on a hike. A whistle weighs almost nothing but could save your life. Always bring safety items before fun items.

outcome.headline: Missing Key Safety Gear!
outcome.explanation: Extra snacks are nice, but you skipped a rain jacket, sunscreen, and a whistle. If it starts raining on the trail, you will get cold and wet with no way to stay dry. Without sunscreen, you could get a painful sunburn. And without a whistle, if you get separated you have no way to signal for help. A tablet will not help you on the trail and could break.
outcome.whatToRemember: Pack SAFETY items first, then fun items if there is room. Water, food, rain layer, sun protection, and a whistle are more important than electronics or extra snacks.
